Periodicity object and shouldn't create one-time billing run. value="If type is <b>Instant start, Manul Start </b>or <b>Start After Process&nbsp;</b>system should create one-time billing run immediately<br><br>When creating one-time billing run with Instant start or Start After Process options, start date should be the current date and time (time + 30 minutes)<br><br>When creating one-time billing run with Manual Start option, start date should be empty" div.div: If defined date is today, then system should create one-time billing run and billing run process will be started when the time comes (Exact Date JOB)

path.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(248, 206, 204), rgb(81, 45, 43)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(184, 84, 80), rgb(215, 129, 126));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 263px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1013px; margin-left: 1929px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: If defined date is in the future (compared to the current day), system should ignore such Process Periodicity object and shouldn't create one-time billing run. It will be created by the Periodic Billing Run JOB

rect.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 268px; height: 1px; padding-top: 58px; margin-left: 1610px;

div.div: One-time billing run will be created on the previous day at 11:59 p.m. by the periodic billing run JOB

path.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(213, 232, 212), rgb(31, 47, 30)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(130, 179, 102), rgb(68, 110, 44));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 338px; height: 1px; padding-top: 521px; margin-left: 1736px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
If type is Instant start, Manul Start or Start After Process system should create one-time billing run immediately
When creating one-time billing run with Instant start or Start After Process options, start date should be the current date and time (time + 30 minutes)
When creating one-time billing run with Manual Start option, start date should be empty

div.div:
For each Process Periodicity object should check and decide if it is possible to create one-time billing run for current day
If system finds more than one relevant periodic object, should use newest one (Biggest ID)
